meta.description: Structural dependency graph from logical axioms to the Standard Model Lagrangian. Each node declares what it presupposes, what it provides to layers above, and what is parametric (swappable without breaking the interface contract).

layers.name: Logic
layers.description: Choice of deductive system. Everything downstream inherits these commitments.

nodes.label: Classical logic
nodes.description: Law of excluded middle, double negation elimination, material conditional. The default substrate for most mathematics.

nodes.label: Intuitionistic logic
nodes.description: Constructive reasoning without LEM. Proofs are programs (BHK interpretation). Naturally typed.

nodes.label: First-order quantification
nodes.description: Universal and existential quantification over individuals. The grammar that lets you state axioms about mathematical objects.

layers.name: Foundations
layers.description: Set-theoretic or type-theoretic substrate. Determines what mathematical objects you can construct.

nodes.label: ZFC set theory
nodes.description: Zermelo-Fraenkel with Choice. The ambient universe for most working mathematics. Provides powersets, infinity, well-ordering.

nodes.label: Type theory
nodes.description: Martin-Löf or variants. Types as propositions, terms as proofs. Foundation for Lean/Coq.

nodes.label: Category theory
nodes.description: Objects, morphisms, functors, natural transformations. The language of structural relationships between mathematical domains.

layers.name: Core algebra
layers.description: Basic algebraic structures. Groups encode symmetry; rings and fields provide arithmetic; linear algebra provides the representation medium.

nodes.label: Group theory
nodes.description: Sets with associative binary operation, identity, inverses. The basic language of symmetry.

nodes.label: Rings & fields
nodes.description: Groups with additional multiplicative structure. Fields add multiplicative inverses for all nonzero elements.

nodes.label: Linear algebra
nodes.description: Vector spaces over fields. Eigenvalues, determinants, tensor products.

layers.name: Topology & analysis
layers.description: Continuity, convergence, completeness. The substrate for geometric and analytic reasoning.

nodes.label: Point-set topology
nodes.description: Open sets, continuity, compactness, connectedness. The substrate for all geometric reasoning.

nodes.label: Real analysis
nodes.description: Completeness of R, limits, measure theory, integration. Dedekind cuts or Cauchy sequences.

nodes.label: Complex analysis
nodes.description: Holomorphic functions, Cauchy integral formula, residues. R² with multiplicative structure.

layers.name: Lie theory
layers.description: Continuous symmetry groups, their infinitesimal structure, and how they act on vector spaces.

nodes.label: Lie groups
nodes.description: Smooth manifolds with compatible group structure. Continuous symmetries.

nodes.label: Lie algebras
nodes.description: Tangent space at identity of a Lie group. Bracket operation. Infinitesimal symmetries.

nodes.label: Representation theory
nodes.description: Homomorphisms from groups/algebras to GL(V). How symmetries act on vector spaces.

layers.name: Differential geometry
layers.description: Manifolds, bundles, connections. The geometric language in which gauge fields live.

nodes.label: Smooth manifolds
nodes.description: Locally Euclidean spaces with smooth transition maps. Charts, atlases, tangent bundles.

nodes.label: Fiber bundles
nodes.description: Spaces locally product but globally twisted. Principal bundles have structure group G acting on fibers.

nodes.label: Connections & curvature
nodes.description: Parallel transport on fiber bundles. Connection 1-form, curvature 2-form. The geometric content of gauge fields.

layers.name: Functional analysis & quantization
layers.description: Infinite-dimensional spaces, operator theory, and the path integral. The bridge from classical to quantum.

nodes.label: Hilbert spaces
nodes.description: Complete inner product spaces. The state space of quantum mechanics.

nodes.label: Operator algebras
nodes.description: C*-algebras, von Neumann algebras. Observables as self-adjoint operators.

nodes.label: Path integral
nodes.description: Sum over histories weighted by exp(iS/hbar). Feynman's formulation. Connects classical action to quantum amplitudes.

layers.name: Classical field theory
layers.description: Lagrangian dynamics on spacetime. Gauge theory as geometry of connections.

nodes.label: Lagrangian / variational
nodes.description: Action functional, Euler-Lagrange equations, Noether's theorem. Classical dynamics from extremization.

nodes.label: Gauge theory (classical)
nodes.description: Yang-Mills theory. Connection on principal G-bundle as gauge field. Curvature = field strength.

layers.name: Quantum field theory
layers.description: Quantized fields, renormalization, anomaly structure. The framework that constrains which classical field theories survive quantization.

nodes.label: Field quantization
nodes.description: Promote fields to operator-valued distributions. Fock space, creation/annihilation operators, normal ordering.

nodes.label: Renormalization
nodes.description: Absorption of UV divergences. Running couplings, RG flow, asymptotic freedom/safety.

nodes.label: Anomaly cancellation
nodes.description: Quantum breaking of classical symmetries. Chiral anomaly, gauge anomaly. Consistency constraints on particle content.
parametric: Fermion content must satisfy trace conditions

layers.name: Standard Model
layers.description: The specific structural configuration that matches observation. Gauge group, matter content, symmetry breaking, flavor mixing.

nodes.label: SU(3)×SU(2)×U(1)
nodes.description: The gauge group. Three simple/abelian factors. Product structure is itself a structural choice — not derived from a single simple group (unless you posit GUT).
parametric: The group itself (why not SU(5)? SO(10)?)

nodes.label: Three fermion generations
nodes.description: Each generation: (Q,u,d,L,e) in specific representations. Three copies. Mass hierarchy across generations unexplained structurally.

nodes.label: Higgs mechanism / SSB
nodes.description: Scalar doublet with Mexican-hat potential. Spontaneous symmetry breaking: SU(2)×U(1)_Y → U(1)_EM. Gives mass to W±, Z, fermions.

nodes.label: Yukawa & mixing
nodes.description: Yukawa couplings generate fermion masses after SSB. CKM (quarks) and PMNS (leptons) encode flavor mixing and CP violation.
